chapter_title.ar: باب اللِّبَاسِ الَّذِي تُكْرَهُ الصَّلَاةُ فِيهِ وَ مَا لَا تُكْرَهُ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 60 - The clothes in which the Salāt is disliked, and what is not disliked

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (15/310)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (15/310)
text: تُكْرَهُ الصَّلَاةُ فِي الْفِرَاءِ إِلَّا مَا صُنِعَ فِي أَرْضِ الْحِجَازِ أَوْ مِمَّا عُلِمَتْ مِنْهُ ذَكَاةٌ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Ibn Abu Umeyr, from Hammad, from Al Halby,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullahasws having said: ‘The Salāt is disliked in the furs except for what is made in the land of Al-Hijaz, or from that which you know it has been purified (slaughtered)’.
en.sarwar: 4. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from ibn abu ‘Umayr from Hammad from al-Halabiy who has said the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘It is undesirable to perform Salah (prayer) in fur except those made in al-Hijaz or that you know is properly slaughtered.’”
